game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: どうぶつしょうぎ is a turn-based digital board game played on a 3x4 grid, where two players control a set of four unique animal pieces. Players tap a piece to select it, then tap a highlighted valid square to move it, aiming to capture the opponent's Lion or move their own Lion into the opponent's back row (Tri). Pieces include the Lion (moves one square in any direction), Giraffe (one square horizontally/vertically), Elephant (one square diagonally), and Chick (one square forward), which promotes to a Chicken (moves one square in any direction except backward diagonally) upon reaching the opponent's back row. Captured pieces can be "dropped" onto any empty square as the current player's piece, adding a strategic layer. A player wins by checkmating the opponent's Lion or by successfully moving their Lion into the Tri zone without immediate threat.
game_info.description: どうぶつしょうぎ (Animal Shogi) is a simplified chess-like game on a 3×4 board. Each player controls 4 animal pieces: Lion (king), Giraffe, Elephant, and Chick. Capture the opponent's Lion or move your Lion to the opponent's back row to win. Captured pieces join your hand and can be dropped back onto the board. The Chick promotes to Chicken when reaching the far row, gaining enhanced movement.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate piece/square selection
SPACE: Select/Confirm piece or move
D: Toggle drop mode to place captured pieces
R: Restart game (from game over screen)
ENTER: Start game (from start screen)
ESC: Pause/Unpause game

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic piece movement, selection, and turn alternation. This test verifies that pieces can be selected, valid moves are highlighted, pieces move correctly, and turns alternate between players.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Select the Chick piece and move it forward one square. Then select the Elephant and move it diagonally. Alternate between both players, making simple legal moves to verify the turn system works correctly.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Pieces should be selectable, valid moves should be highlighted in yellow, pieces should move to selected squares, and the turn indicator should alternate between "Your Turn" and "Opponent's Turn". The test succeeds when multiple pieces have moved and turns alternate properly.

TEST_1.strategy: Select the Chick piece and move it forward one square. Then select the Elephant and move it diagonally. Alternate between both players, making simple legal moves to verify the turn system works correctly.
TEST_1.expected: Pieces should be selectable, valid moves should be highlighted in yellow, pieces should move to selected squares, and the turn indicator should alternate between "Your Turn" and "Opponent's Turn". The test succeeds when multiple pieces have moved and turns alternate properly.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing win condition by moving the Lion to the opponent's back row (Tri zone). This verifies that the game correctly detects when a Lion reaches the promotion zone and declares victory.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Move pieces to create a path for the Lion to advance. Systematically move the Lion forward toward the opponent's back row (row 0 for player 1, row 3 for player 2). Once the Lion reaches the opponent's promotion zone, the game should end.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The game should detect when the Lion enters the opponent's back row and transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state, displaying a victory message. The test succeeds when the win condition is properly triggered and the game over screen appears.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing piece capture mechanics and the captured pieces hand system. This verifies that when a piece captures an opponent's piece, it is added to the capturing player's hand.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Maneuver pieces to create capture opportunities. Move a piece to a square occupied by an opponent's piece to capture it. Verify that the captured piece appears in the capturing player's hand display.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: When a piece captures an opponent's piece, the captured piece should be removed from the board and added to the capturing player's hand. The hand display should update to show the captured piece. The test succeeds when captures work correctly and the hand updates properly.
TEST_3.description: Testing piece capture mechanics and the captured pieces hand system. This verifies that when a piece captures an opponent's piece, it is added to the capturing player's hand.
TEST_3.strategy: Maneuver pieces to create capture opportunities. Move a piece to a square occupied by an opponent's piece to capture it. Verify that the captured piece appears in the capturing player's hand display.
TEST_3.expected: When a piece captures an opponent's piece, the captured piece should be removed from the board and added to the capturing player's hand. The hand display should update to show the captured piece. The test succeeds when captures work correctly and the hand updates properly.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing the drop mechanic where captured pieces can be placed back on the board. This verifies that players can select a piece from their hand and place it on an empty square.
TEST_4.strategy_description: After capturing an opponent's piece (from TEST_3), press D to enter drop mode. Select a piece from the hand, then select an empty square on the board to place it. Verify the piece is placed and belongs to the current player.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Pressing D should toggle drop mode. When a piece from the hand is selected, empty squares should be highlighted. Placing the piece should remove it from the hand and add it to the board under the current player's control. The test succeeds when pieces can be dropped successfully.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing Chick promotion to Chicken when reaching the opponent's back row. This verifies that the Chick piece transforms into a Chicken with enhanced movement capabilities.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Move a Chick piece forward toward the opponent's back row. Once the Chick reaches row 0 (for player 1) or row 3 (for player 2), it should automatically promote to a Chicken. Test the Chicken's movement to verify it has the correct move set.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: When a Chick reaches the opponent's back row, it should visually change to indicate promotion (Chicken has a different appearance). The promoted piece should have enhanced movement (one square in any direction except backward diagonally). The test succeeds when promotion occurs automatically and the new piece has correct movement.

TEST_5.expected: When a Chick reaches the opponent's back row, it should visually change to indicate promotion (Chicken has a different appearance). The promoted piece should have enhanced movement (one square in any direction except backward diagonally). The test succeeds when promotion occurs automatically and the new piece has correct movement.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing Lion capture win condition. This verifies that capturing the opponent's Lion immediately ends the game with a victory for the capturing player.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Maneuver pieces to create an opportunity to capture the opponent's Lion. Move a piece to the square occupied by the opponent's Lion to capture it. The game should immediately end in victory.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: When the opponent's Lion is captured, the game should immediately transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state and display a victory message. The test succeeds when Lion capture is properly detected and ends the game.
TEST_6.description: Testing Lion capture win condition. This verifies that capturing the opponent's Lion immediately ends the game with a victory for the capturing player.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing movement validation and illegal move prevention. This verifies that pieces cannot move to invalid squares and that movement rules are enforced correctly for each piece type.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Attempt to select pieces and try to move them to invalid squares (out of bounds, occupied by friendly pieces, or not in the piece's valid move set). Verify that invalid moves are not highlighted and cannot be executed.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Only valid moves should be highlighted when a piece is selected. Attempting to move to invalid squares should have no effect. Pieces should respect their movement patterns (Lion: 1 square any direction, Giraffe: 1 square orthogonal, Elephant: 1 square diagonal, Chick: 1 square forward, Chicken: 1 square any except back diagonal). The test succeeds when all movement restrictions are properly enforced.
